text:
Once upon a time in a quaint town, nestled between rolling hills and verdant forests, there was a school called Harmony Heights. Known for its thriving community hall run by the dedicated Mrs. Clara, the school was a hub of laughter, learning, and endless mischief.

The story begins on a sunny afternoon when the last bell rang. The students, a bright ensemble of children, dashed out of the school, their laughter echoing through the corridors. Among them was Tommy, a curious fifth-grader who had an unusual passion for making things erupt with electricity.

Tommy’s fascination came not from sparking fires, but from generating harmless electrical sparks in the air, creating miniature lightning storms with his unique knack. This had gotten him in a bit of trouble more than once, but Mrs. Clara always explained the importance of learning from ones' blunders rather than fearing them.
